topic_title: Every API call is a design decision — latency, cost, and reliability considered upfront

soft_findings: §5.4 line 161: 'sixty times more expensive than Haiku' — no citation marker on that exact sentence (same fact is cited in §5.2 and §7; not a fabrication, minor omission).
§6 code block comment says 'Source: Anthropic pricing page' in plain text rather than a [N] marker — numerical constants in code are not prose assertions; acceptable but noted.

sequence_drift.detail: All API and Python terms (client.messages.create, message.usage, message.content[0].text, try/except, time.sleep, RateLimitError, APIConnectionError, AuthenticationError, BadRequestError) introduced in 12.1–12.13. New concepts introduced here: latency, token pricing, input/output token cost formula, model-tier trade-off, Haiku/Sonnet/Opus, three-question design checklist, cost-and-latency note, time.time(). Batch API and prompt caching are named-and-deferred in one sentence each with explicit 'not in scope for this module' pointer — compliant with mention-and-defer rule. No prompt engineering (13.x) or RAG/agents (14.x) forward references found.

length_sanity.detail: Estimated ~2100 words (prose + inline code comments). Within atomic corpus range of 1500–2400w. Comfortably above hard-fail floor of 1050w.

topic_scope.detail: All five subsections (5.1 Latency, 5.2 Cost, 5.3 Reliability, 5.4 Model tiers, 5.5 Three-question checklist) and the §6 cost-and-latency note implementation are squarely on the headline. Batch API and prompt caching are mention-and-defer only ('mentioned here so you know they exist; they are not in scope for this module'). No prompt engineering techniques, RAG, or agent frameworks taught. Scope is clean.

soft_findings.detail: §5.1: 'Larger models (more parameters, more capability)' — 'parameters' (ML model weight count) is used without a plain-language definition. Used in passing context; meaning is inferable from surrounding sentence. Single instance, below the ≥2 undefined-terms soft-fail threshold.

beginner_readability.detail: Introduction and Core Concepts are written in plain, direct, second-person prose. Terms defined on first use: Latency (§5.1), Tokens (§5.2), Rate limits (§5.3) with RPM/TPM spelled out inline. One borderline term ('parameters') used without definition but in a context that does not impede comprehension. No jargon dumps (no paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously). Sentence lengths are predominantly under 20 words. No hedging phrases detected. Total soft findings: 1 — below the ≥3 escalation threshold.

verdict: PASS — all 6 checks passed. Primary resource [1] (artificialanalysis.ai) is cited in 8 sections; citation density across §5/6/7 is ~89% (threshold 60%). No forward references to 13.x or 14.x material; batch API and prompt caching are correctly mention-and-deferred. Word count ~2100w is within atomic corpus range 1500–2400w. Sections 3, 4, 5, 10 are all present and non-trivial. Scope is clean — no scope_creep findings. One beginner-readability soft finding (ML 'parameters' used without plain-language gloss in §5.1) — below escalation threshold. Corpus is ready to ground downstream artifacts.
